name: controller_manager_msgs
description: Messages and services for the controller manager.

ChainConnection.raw:
string name        # name of controller exporting reference_interfaces
string[] reference_interfaces        # list of reference exported by controller

fields.name: reference_interfaces
fields.comment: list of reference exported by controller

ControllerState.raw:
string name        # controller name
string state        # controller state: unconfigured, inactive, active, or finalized
string type        # the controller class name, e.g. joint_trajectory_controller/JointTrajectoryController
string[] claimed_interfaces        # command interfaces currently owned by controller
string[] required_command_interfaces        # command interfaces required by controller
string[] required_state_interfaces        # state interfaces required by controller
bool is_chainable        # specifies whether or not controller can export references for a controller chain
bool is_chained        # specifies whether or not controller's exported references are claimed by another controller
string[] reference_interfaces        # references to be exported (only applicable if is_chainable is true)
ChainConnection[] chain_connections        # specifies list of controllers and their exported references that the controller is chained to

fields.name: state
fields.comment: controller state: unconfigured, inactive, active, or finalized

fields.name: claimed_interfaces
fields.comment: command interfaces currently owned by controller

fields.name: is_chainable
fields.comment: specifies whether or not controller can export references for a controller chain

fields.name: is_chained
fields.comment: specifies whether or not controller's exported references are claimed by another controller

fields.name: reference_interfaces
fields.comment: references to be exported (only applicable if is_chainable is true)

fields.name: chain_connections
fields.comment: specifies list of controllers and their exported references that the controller is chained to

HardwareComponentState.raw:
string name
string type
string class_type
lifecycle_msgs/State state
HardwareInterface[] command_interfaces
HardwareInterface[] state_interfaces

HardwareInterface.raw:
string name
bool is_available
bool is_claimed

ConfigureController.description: The ConfigureController service allows you to configure a single controller
ConfigureController.raw:
# The ConfigureController service allows you to configure a single controller
# inside controller_manager

# To configure a controller, specify the "name" of the controller.
# The return value "ok" indicates if the controller was successfully
# configured or not.

string name
---
bool ok

ListControllerTypes.description: The ListControllers service returns a list of controller types that are known
ListControllerTypes.raw:
# The ListControllers service returns a list of controller types that are known
# to the controller manager plugin mechanism.

---
string[] types
string[] base_classes

ListControllers.description: The ListControllers service returns a list of controller names/states/types of the
ListControllers.raw:
# The ListControllers service returns a list of controller names/states/types of the
# controllers that are loaded inside the controller_manager.

---
controller_manager_msgs/ControllerState[] controller

ListHardwareComponents.description: The ListHardwareComponents service returns a list of hardware HardwareComponentsState.
ListHardwareComponents.raw:
# The ListHardwareComponents service returns a list of hardware HardwareComponentsState.
# This will convey name, component_type, state and type of the components
# that are loaded inside the resource_manager.

---
HardwareComponentState[] component

LoadController.description: The LoadController service allows you to load a single controller
LoadController.raw:
# The LoadController service allows you to load a single controller
# inside controller_manager

# To load a controller, specify the "name" of the controller.
# The return value "ok" indicates if the controller was successfully
# constructed and initialized or not.

string name
---
bool ok

ReloadControllerLibraries.description: The ReloadControllerLibraries service will reload all controllers that are available in
ReloadControllerLibraries.raw:
# The ReloadControllerLibraries service will reload all controllers that are available in
# the system as plugins

# Reloading libraries only works if there are no controllers loaded. If there
# are still some controllers loaded, the reloading will fail.
# If this bool is set to true, all loaded controllers will get
# killed automatically, and the reloading can succeed.
bool force_kill
---
bool ok

SetHardwareComponentState.description: The SetHardwareComponentState service allows to control life-cycle of a single hardware component.
SetHardwareComponentState.raw:
# The SetHardwareComponentState service allows to control life-cycle of a single hardware component.
# Supported states are defined in the design document of LifecycleNodes available at:
# https://design.ros2.org/articles/node_lifecycle.html
# To control life-cycle of a hardware component, specify its "name" and "target_state".
# Target state may be defined by "id" using a constant from `lifecycle_msgs/msg/State` or a label
# using definitions from `hardware_interface/types/lifecycle_state_names.hpp` file.
# The return value "ok" indicates if the component has successfully changed its state to "target_state".
# The return value "state" returns current state of the hardware component.

string name
lifecycle_msgs/State target_state
---
bool ok
lifecycle_msgs/State state

SwitchController.description: The SwitchController service allows you to deactivate a number of controllers
SwitchController.raw:
# The SwitchController service allows you to deactivate a number of controllers
# and activate a number of controllers, all in one single timestep of the
# controller manager's control loop.

# To switch controllers, specify
#  * the list of controller names to activate,
#  * the list of controller names to deactivate, and
#  * the strictness (BEST_EFFORT or STRICT)
#    * STRICT means that switching will fail if anything goes wrong (an invalid
#      controller name, a controller that failed to activate, etc. )
#    * BEST_EFFORT means that even when something goes wrong with one controller,
#      the service will still try to activate/stop the remaining controllers
#  * activate the controllers as soon as their hardware dependencies are ready, will
#    wait for all interfaces to be ready otherwise
#  * the timeout before aborting pending controllers. Zero for infinite

# The return value "ok" indicates if the controllers were switched
# successfully or not.  The meaning of success depends on the
# specified strictness.


string[] activate_controllers
string[] deactivate_controllers
string[] start_controllers       # DEPRECATED: Use activate_controllers filed instead
string[] stop_controllers        # DEPRECATED: Use deactivate_controllers filed instead
int32 strictness
int32 BEST_EFFORT=1
int32 STRICT=2
bool start_asap                 # DEPRECATED: Use activate_asap filed instead
bool activate_asap
builtin_interfaces/Duration timeout
---
bool ok

UnloadController.description: The UnloadController service allows you to unload a single controller
UnloadController.raw:
# The UnloadController service allows you to unload a single controller
# from controller_manager

# To unload a controller, specify the "name" of the controller.
# The return value "ok" indicates if the controller was successfully
# unloaded or not

string name
---
bool ok
